content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the structure. Atom O:10 is the red sphere at the bottom, and it is connected to N:9 (the blue nitrogen atom in the center of the nitrate group).

Based on my visual inspection of the molecule from multiple angles, I can confidently report the neighbour atoms of atom 10:

## Results

**Neighbour atoms of atom 10 (O:10):**

| Atomic Index | Element |
|--------------|---------|
| 9            | N       |

**geomconf_index:** 3447

**Explanation:** Atom O:10 is part of a nitrate group (-NO₃). It forms a covalent bond only with the central nitrogen atom N:9. The nitrate group consists of N:9 as the central atom bonded to three oxygen atoms: O:8, O:10, and O:11.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 10, whose element should be O. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
